---
title: "Baseline experiment: resting state and the Dolan agonist transient"
subtitle: "Platelet whole-cell model — demo experiment 1 of 4"
author: "Steve Haigh"
date: 2026-06-23
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm"
    fontsize: 11pt
    mainfont: "STIX Two Text"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Purpose

This is the **baseline** run — the unperturbed reference that the perturbation
experiments are compared against. It does two things:

1. shows that the model holds a **stable resting state**, and
2. reproduces an **agonist-evoked Ca²⁺ transient** that *appears similar to* that
   of Dolan & Diamond (*Biophys. J.* 2014) when a physiological agonist mixture
   is applied after a 60 s settling period, and shows that the activated state is
   **maintained** out to 300 s.

The perturbation experiments reuse this exact set-up and change one model setting
at a time, reading the difference against the reference values established here.

## Model set-up

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Cell | single platelet, +extracellular Ca²⁺ (1.2 mM) |
| Agonists | thrombin 1 nM, ADP 10 µM, ATP 10 µM (the Dolan standard) |
| Agonist onset | t = 60 s (a 60 s settle precedes stimulation) |
| Duration | 300 s |
| Model settings | `agonist_delay_s = 60`; all other parameters, counts and rate constants left at their defaults |

The agonists enter as transient time-courses (a rise and a slow decay), not step
inputs — this matches the *shape* of an agonist bolus reaching a platelet, though
the precise time-course is a modelling choice, not a measurement. The exact
commands to reproduce this run and its figures are in the [Appendix](#appendix).

## Resting state

Started from the Dolan initial conditions (cytosolic Ca²⁺ = 100 nM, dense
tubular system (DTS) store = 250 µM), the model does **not** sit exactly still:
those initial values are not its own equilibrium, so it undergoes a damped
settling transient — an overshoot to ~128 nM by t ≈ 30 s — before converging to
its resting fixed point of **~95 nM cytosolic / ~255 µM store** by ~300 s
(@fig-resting). Resting IP₃ holds at ~50 nM throughout.

![**Resting state with no agonist (300 s).** Left: cytosolic Ca²⁺ relaxes from the imposed initial value (100 nM) through a damped overshoot (~128 nM at ~30 s) to a maintained fixed point of ~95 nM. Centre: the DTS store mirrors this (250 → ~199 → ~255 µM); store-operated entry stays near its low resting level. Right: IP₃ holds at ~50 nM. The model converges to and then holds a resting steady state close to the Dolan resting targets (100 nM / 250 µM); the settling takes a few hundred seconds because the imposed initial values are not the model's exact equilibrium.](../figures/demos/resting_calcium.png){#fig-resting width=100%}

The practical consequence: at the 60 s mark used below (and by Dolan), the
resting state is still completing this settle. This does not materially affect
the agonist response, but it is the honest reason the resting trace in
@fig-agonist is not perfectly flat over 0–60 s.

## Agonist response

Applying the agonist mixture at t = 60 s evokes the canonical response
(@fig-agonist): cytosolic Ca²⁺ rises and is sustained as a **plateau of
~423 nM** (held from ~150 s to 300 s), the DTS store empties (~209 → ~1 µM),
store-operated entry switches on, and IP₃ rises to ~220 nM. Downstream, the
αIIbβ3 integrin reaches a PAC-1⁺ active fraction of ~0.80 and thromboxane A₂
rises to ~1 µM.

![**Resting state then Dolan agonist transient (agonist at 60 s, 300 s total).** Left: cytosolic Ca²⁺ — the brief resting settle (0–60 s) is followed by a rise to a sustained ~423 nM plateau maintained to 300 s. Centre: the DTS store briefly *over-fills* at agonist onset (~209 → ~237 µM at t ≈ 61 s; explained below) and then empties (→ ~1 µM) as the IP₃ receptors open, while store-operated Ca²⁺ entry (right axis, dashed) switches on to sustain the cytosolic plateau. Right: endogenously generated IP₃ rises to ~220 nM. The sustained (rather than transient-then-recovering) plateau is the model's autocrine "second wave" under a saturating agonist; it is store-limited, so the peak height is set by buffering and SOCE rather than by the exact agonist dose.](../figures/demos/baseline_calcium.png){#fig-agonist width=100%}

### The store transiently rises at agonist onset

The brief upward spike in the DTS store at ~60 s (centre panel of @fig-agonist)
is worth noting. It is a **multi-timescale** effect. At onset the fast,
ATP-gated **P2X1** channel admits Ca²⁺ almost immediately — cytosolic Ca²⁺ jumps
from ~122 to ~175 nM within a second — while **IP₃ is still low** (~50 → ~55 nM)
because the slower Gq → PLCβ → IP₃ cascade has not yet built up. SERCA pumps that
early Ca²⁺ influx into the store, so the store briefly **over-fills**
(~209 → ~237 µM) before it is drained. A few seconds later IP₃ crosses threshold
(~60–70 nM), the IP₃ receptors open, and the store discharges to ~1 µM. The
spike is therefore the model correctly separating the rapid ionotropic (P2X1)
response from the slower receptor → IP₃ pathway, rather than an artefact.

## Comparison with real platelets

- **Resting Ca²⁺.** Real resting platelets sit at ~50–100 nM cytosolic Ca²⁺ with
  a high DTS store; the model's resting fixed point (~95 nM / ~255 µM) is within
  this range and close to the Dolan targets. The damped settling transient is a
  consequence of the chosen initial values, not a biological prediction.
- **Agonist transient.** Real platelets respond to thrombin/ADP with a rapid
  cytosolic Ca²⁺ rise to several hundred nM, sustained in the presence of
  extracellular Ca²⁺ by store-operated entry — qualitatively what the model
  shows, and the basis of its validation against Dolan & Diamond (2014) Fig. 4
  (the "5/5" peak / SOCE-differential criteria).
- **Honest differences.** (i) The model's store empties almost completely
  (~1 µM), whereas Dolan infer a substantial residual; the free luminal store
  Ca²⁺ has never been measured directly, so this is a falsifiable model
  prediction, not a known error. (ii) The real transient is sharper and shows
  more recovery; the model's sustained plateau reflects its autocrine
  amplification loops under a saturating agonist. (iii) Cytosolic free Ca²⁺ is
  **store-limited** in the model — the peak is nearly insensitive to most single
  parameters — which is the key caveat for interpreting any perturbation of this
  baseline.

## Provenance

The simulations were produced by `runscripts/manual/runFromConfig.py`, and the
figures by `runscripts/manual/analysisPlatelet.py --plot demo_calcium` (figure
module `models/platelet/analysis/single/demo_calcium.py`, reading the
`CalciumTrace` listener). Both runs used **seed 0** for **300 s** on Python
3.11.5, at git commit **`9724cc81`**. The exact commit is also stamped in the
footnote of each figure and in the run's `metadata/metadata.json`, so a figure
remains traceable to the code that produced it even when separated from this
document.

## Take-away

The model holds a physiologically reasonable resting state and reproduces an
agonist transient and maintained activated plateau that appear similar to Dolan
& Diamond (2014). It serves as the unperturbed reference: the perturbation
experiments change individual model settings and read the difference against the
values reported here.

## Appendix — reproduce {#appendix}

Run the experiments and produce the figures from the repository root
(`PYTHONPATH=$PWD`):

```bash
# Fig. 1 — resting state: all three agonists held at zero, 300 s
cat > rest.json <<'EOF'
{"length_sec": 300, "seed": 0,
 "run_config": {"thrombin_peak_nM": 0, "adp_peak_uM": 0, "atp_ex_peak_uM": 0}}
EOF
python runscripts/manual/runFromConfig.py --config rest.json --out exp_rest
MPLBACKEND=Agg python runscripts/manual/analysisPlatelet.py exp_rest --plot demo_calcium

# Fig. 2 — resting + Dolan agonist applied at 60 s (the baseline)
cat > base.json <<'EOF'
{"length_sec": 300, "seed": 0, "run_config": {"agonist_delay_s": 60}}
EOF
python runscripts/manual/runFromConfig.py --config base.json --out exp_base
MPLBACKEND=Agg python runscripts/manual/analysisPlatelet.py exp_base --plot demo_calcium
```

`run_config` fields map onto `reconstruction/platelet/run_config.py`; omitted
agonist peaks fall back to the Dolan standard (thrombin 1 nM, ADP 10 µM,
ATP 10 µM). The figure module
`models/platelet/analysis/single/demo_calcium.py` (shared by all demo-experiment
figures) plots the `CalciumTrace` columns `ca_cyt_nM`, `ca_dts_uM`,
`soce_flux_nMs` and `ip3_nM`.
